title: Cellebrite UFED mobile extraction
action: Connect the device to the UFED workstation, document make/model/IMEI/serial in the extraction log, and pick the highest-fidelity extraction the device supports (Full File System where available, then Advanced Logical, then Logical). Capture the extraction in UFED's case-bound output directory; do not let UFED write to the original device.
rationale: UFED's extraction tier dictates what artefacts you'll see downstream: Full File System unlocks app sandbox data and decrypted iOS keychain entries that Logical can't reach. Picking the wrong tier without documenting why creates an integrity question at trial.
expected_result: UFED extraction completes; output written as a UFDR / .zip alongside the device-info report; computed hash recorded in UFED's case log and in the chain-of-custody form.
references: Cellebrite Forensic Methodology — Mobile Device Extraction
SWGDE Best Practices for Mobile Device Evidence Preservation and Acquisition

cellebrite.action: UFED 4PC / Touch → Extract → Mobile Device → choose vendor + model. Use the picker's connection guide for cable + mode (DFU, ADB enabled, etc.). Pick extraction type per the tier ladder above; under iOS use Checkm8 / Advanced Logical as available. Output goes to the case folder; record the computed hash from the post-extraction summary screen.
cellebrite.ui_path: UFED 4PC > Extract > Mobile Device > [vendor] > [model] > Extraction Type
cellebrite.notes: Airplane Mode + Faraday bag the device before attaching; a partial OTA update mid-extraction will tank the timeline. iOS 16+ devices may need supervised mode or Checkm8 for FFS — log the chosen path in the extraction notes.
